evals.prompt: Can you crawl our site and find the technical SEO problems across our pages? Want a baseline I can come back to.
evals.expected_output: Should use report id site-crawl to crawl linked pages, check technical SEO signals, and save a local baseline with a saved crawl id for follow-up. Should follow the discovery flow: seo_describe_report with id site-crawl, then seo_run_report with the required url and a bounded maxPages. Should read status, requestEvidenceStatus, attempted and fetched counts, failures, retained pages, and pageLimitReached first, then data-source states, then warnings and caveats, with pages, requests, and issues kept opt-in. Should preserve the saved crawl id for later filtering or comparison.
assertions: Uses report id site-crawl to crawl and save a baseline
Passes the required url and a bounded maxPages
Reads status, requestEvidenceStatus, attempted, fetched, failures, and pageLimitReached first
Keeps pages, requests, and issues opt-in rather than dumping them
Preserves the saved crawl id for later filtering or comparison

evals.prompt: Crawl https://example.com with maxPages 200, maxDepth 3, and js true, then hand me the highest-priority fixes.
evals.expected_output: Should run report id site-crawl with url https://example.com, maxPages 200, maxDepth 3, and js true, via seo reports run site-crawl or seo_run_report. Should read status, attempted and fetched counts, failures, retained pages, and pageLimitReached, then route to report id top-fixes against the saved crawl to rank the queue. Should note that if pageLimitReached is true the crawl was capped, and that a crawl is not an index, traffic source, or guarantee of complete site coverage.
assertions: Runs report id site-crawl with maxPages 200, maxDepth 3, and js true
Reads status, attempted, fetched, failures, retained pages, and pageLimitReached
Routes to report id top-fixes against the saved crawl to rank fixes
States a crawl is not an index, traffic source, or complete coverage
Uses only parameters defined in the report schema

evals.prompt: The crawl finished and found no critical issues. That means the whole site is clean and every page is fine, right?
evals.expected_output: Should refuse to read a finished crawl as a sitewide all-clear. Should explain that a partial or capped crawl cannot support an all-clear or a definitive zero, and check pageLimitReached, attempted versus fetched counts, and failures before concluding. Should state that if JavaScript rendering was disabled that is a discovery boundary, and recommend preserving the saved crawl id and re-running the same configuration to confirm any change.
assertions: Refuses to read the finished crawl as a sitewide all-clear
States a partial or capped crawl cannot support an all-clear or a definitive zero
Checks pageLimitReached, attempted versus fetched counts, and failures
States a disabled JavaScript rendering is a discovery boundary
Recommends re-running the same configuration to confirm a change
